# chore: release v2.14.0 — runtime durable execution + scheduler dispatch

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `8706c4b60be5` — chore: release v2.14.0 — runtime durable execution + scheduler dispatch

**Body**:

Cuts releases for three packages with unreleased commits on main:

- kailash 2.13.5 → 2.14.0 (minor)
  - LocalRuntime/AsyncLocalRuntime checkpoint hook (#860)
  - WorkflowScheduler dispatch_via= pluggable dispatcher (#859)
  - W1/W3 hardening: bounded LRU, narrowed exception handling,
    fail-CLOSED on policy errors, security review fixes
- kailash-dataflow 2.8.0 → 2.8.1 (patch)
  - #857 AppendOnlyForbiddenNode __new__ bypass closure (PR #868)
  - bulk_upsert defense-in-depth comment
- kailash-mcp 0.2.11 → 0.2.12 (patch)
  - ErrorAggregator.get_error_trends() do-while semantics fix
    (orphan from commit 5ca2f993, caught at /release-time scope
    enumeration per build-repo-release-discipline.md MUST Rule 5)

All 8 framework packages bump kailash>= pin from 2.13.4 to 2.14.0
per release-checklist § "SDK Dependency Pin Update Rule".

README ecosystem-version table updated to current PyPI versions
(was stale at Kaizen v1.2.5 / Nexus v1.4.2 / DataFlow v0.12.4 vs
actual PyPI 2.20.0 / 2.6.2 / 2.8.0); two new framework rows added
(MCP, PACT, ML, Align had no entries).

Sphinx api/runtime.rst gains a Durable Execution & Distributed
Dispatch section with autodoc for the new dispatcher.py + durable.py
modules. Sphinx build verified locally with 0 errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
